title: Hieracium mnoocladum
taxonomy: Hieracium mnoocladum is a species of plant in the dandelion tribe within the daisy family. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Angiospermophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, and the order Asterales. This plant is further classified into the family Asteraceae, the subfamily Cichorioideae, and the tribe Cichorieae. The genus Hieracium is a large and complex group of plants, often referred to as hawkweeds. As a member of this genus, Hieracium mnoocladum shares characteristics with other hawkweeds, such as similar growth habits and flower structures.

intro: Hieracium mnoocladum, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Hieracium mnoocladum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and pro-inflammatory activities. In herbal medicine, the plant is often made into infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is used to treat conditions such as eczema, acne, and digestive issues.
